commit_message:
Converting UMI to a communication stack Converting UMI to a communication stack This PR converts UMI from a single layer merged protocol, transaction, link, phy standard into a layered protocol stack for the sake of modularity. A shared memory approach is applicable to chips, chiplets, sips, and systems and as we grow out of the on-chip domain, we need to be able to add layers to the stack (just like ethernet, PCIE,etc).

The initial insipration for this PR came out of the move from 3D to 2D/1

Converting UMI to a communication stack Converting UMI to a communication stack This PR converts UMI from a single layer merged protocol, transaction, link, phy standard into a layered protocol stack for the sake of modularity. A shared memory approach is applicable to chips, chiplets, sips, and systems and as we grow out of the on-chip domain, we need to be able to add layers to the stack (just like ethernet, PCIE,etc).

examples.patch:
@@ -5,7 +5,10 @@
  *
  * Documentation:
  *
- * The input request vector is a concatenated onenot vectors of inputs
+ * The crossbar is pipelined and non-blocking, allowing for any-to-any
+ * simulataneous input to output connections.
+ *
+ * The input request vector is a concatenated vectors of inputs
  * requesting outputs ports per the order below.
  *
  * [0]     = input 0   requesting output 0
@@ -18,6 +21,9 @@
  * [2*N-1] = input N-1 requesting output 1
  * ...
  *
+ * Input to output paths are enabled through the [NxN] wide 'mask' input,
+ * which follows ordering of the input valid convention shown above.
+ *
  ******************************************************************************/
 module umi_crossbar
   #(parameter TARGET = "DEFAULT", // implementation target
